my_thoughts: The rejection works: must start as we are (cannot wait till we change), mechanical change = worse. The student is setting impossible standard (proposes precondition), which shows they're seeking perfectionist rule. They'll likely ask about scale next. Hypothesis: precondition rejections trigger scale questions—testable by tracking whether precondition rejections lead to scale inquiries.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student proposes precondition ('Is psychological thinking only possible when all our functions are working rightly?')—setting impossible standard via proposes precondition and seeks perfectionist rule. They're seeking perfectionist rule (all functions working = precondition). The constraint_collapse mechanism rejects: must start as we are (cannot wait till we change), mechanical change = worse. Pattern: precondition question → rejection → [next: scale question]. Medium confidence because precondition rejections typically trigger clarification requests.
